core_technical_requirements: Deep technical depth in modern ML systems
Production experience with embeddings-based retrieval systems (e.g., sentence-transformers, OpenAI embeddings, BGE, E5)
Production experience with vector databases or hybrid search infrastructure (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus, OpenSearch, Elasticsearch, FAISS)
Hands-on experience designing evaluation frameworks for ranking systems (e.g., NDCG, MRR, MAP, offline-to-online correlation, A/B test interpretation)
Setting up evaluation infrastructure (offline benchmarks, online A/B testing, recruiter-feedback loops)
hidden_text_markers: Willing to ship suboptimal code quickly to learn from real users
Comfortable with a role where the JD changes every six months
Willing to think about product, recruiter workflows, and evaluation frameworks
Capable of operating in both 'researcher' and 'shipper' modes, with a slight tilt towards 'shipper'
Culture-fit matters more than skills-fit at this stage
Works async-first and writes a lot
Moves fast and breaks internal assumptions
Has strong opinions about retrieval (hybrid vs dense), evaluation (offline vs online), and LLM integration (when to fine-tune vs prompt)
Can defend opinions with reference to systems they actually built
explicit_anti_patterns: Candidates seeking well-scoped roles with defined ladders (e.g., from Google or Meta)
Candidates who want to 'just code' without thinking about product or workflows
Candidates whose career has been spent in pure research environments (academic labs, research-only roles) without production deployment
Candidates whose 'AI experience' is primarily recent (under 12 months) projects using LangChain to call OpenAI, without substantial pre-LLM-era ML production experience
Senior engineers who haven't written production code in the last 18 months due to 'architecture' or 'tech lead' roles
Title-chasers optimizing for promotions by switching companies every 1.5 years
Framework enthusiasts whose primary focus is on using hot frameworks rather than systems thinking
Candidates whose entire career has been at consulting firms (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Accenture, Cognizant, Capgemini, etc.) without prior product-company experience
Candidates whose primary expertise is computer vision, speech, or robotics without significant NLP/IR exposure
Candidates whose work has been entirely on closed-source proprietary systems for 5+ years without external validation (papers, talks, open-source)
Candidates who need a stable, mature codebase to be productive
optional_preferred_requirements: Experience with learning-to-rank models (XGBoost-based or neural)
Prior exposure to HR-tech, recruiting tech, or marketplace products
Background in distributed systems or large-scale inference optimization
Open-source contributions in the AI/ML space

availability_requirements: Willingness to buy out up to 30 days of notice period
Active on Redrob platform or clear signal of being in the job market
Willingness for quarterly travel for offsites

algorithmic_rules.keyword_trap_mitigation_strategy: Avoid keyword traps by reasoning about the gap between what the JD says and what it means. A candidate's career history building a recommendation system at a product company is a fit, even without specific AI keywords. A candidate with all AI keywords but an irrelevant title (e.g., Marketing Manager) is not a fit. Focus on demonstrated experience and impact over keyword matching.
behavioural_signals: Low activity on platform (e.g., hasn't logged in for 6 months)
Not actively in the job market

synthetic_ideal_candidate_embedding_stri: I am a Senior AI Engineer with 6-8 years of total experience, including 4-5 years in applied ML/AI roles at product companies, not pure services. I have successfully shipped at least one end-to-end ranking, search, or recommendation system to real users at meaningful scale, demonstrating deep technical depth in modern ML systems including embeddings, retrieval, ranking, LLMs, and fine-tuning. I possess production experience with embeddings-based retrieval systems (e.g., sentence-transformers, OpenAI embeddings, BGE, E5) and vector databases (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, Milvus, OpenSearch, Elasticsearch, FAISS), with a proven ability to handle embedding drift, index refresh, and retrieval-quality regression in production. My strong Python skills are complemented by hands-on experience designing and interpreting evaluation frameworks for ranking systems, such as NDCG, MRR, MAP, offline-to-online correlation, and A/B testing. I embody a scrappy product-engineering attitude, prioritizing shipping quickly to learn from users, even if the initial ML solution is suboptimal, and I am comfortable with the dynamic nature of a Series A startup. I have strong, defensible opinions on retrieval strategies (hybrid vs dense), evaluation methodologies (offline vs online), and LLM integration (fine-tuning vs prompting), backed by systems I've personally built. I am a proactive problem-solver, capable of driving long-term architecture, mentoring junior engineers, and collaborating closely with product managers. I thrive in an async-first, writing-heavy environment where open disagreement leads to quick decisions, and I am comfortable with a fast-paced, evolving codebase. I am located in or willing to relocate to Noida or Pune, and I am actively engaged in the job market.
